name: Tutorial
description: A guided walkthrough of the DTID kill chain. A single slow-moving quadcopter approaches while step-by-step instructions guide you through detection, tracking, identification, and defeat.

tutorial_prompts.message: Welcome to OpenSentry. A drone is approaching your base. Watch the tactical map for a new contact to appear.
Contact detected! The yellow square on the map is an UNKNOWN track. Click on it to select it, then click CONFIRM TRACK in the engagement panel.
Track confirmed. Sensors are now correlating data. Watch the confidence indicator rise. When you have enough data, click IDENTIFY to classify the threat.
Confidence is building. Select the correct classification (Commercial Quad) and affiliation (Hostile), then confirm identification.

Threat identified! Now select an effector to engage. You have several options: RF/PNT Jammer (low collateral, rechargeable), DE-LASER-3km (high precision, requires line of sight), or JACKAL (kinetic, limited ammo). The Jammer or DE Laser are optimal for this target.

Target neutralized! Well done. You completed the DTID kill chain: Detect, Track, Identify, Defeat. The DE-LASER-3km you used is a precision weapon — high kill probability against individual drones with low collateral risk. In harder scenarios, you'll also have access to DE-HPM-3km, a wide-area pulse weapon that dominates swarm attacks. Review your debrief score.

roe_briefing: Positive identification required before any engagement
Do not engage tracks classified as FRIENDLY or CIVILIAN
Kinetic engagement authorized only after electronic countermeasures fail or are unavailable
All engagements must be within designated weapon engagement zones (WEZ)
Report all engagements to Battle Captain immediately
